prs.title: docs(wallet): add regtest journey-state guide and friction audit
prs.body:
## What does this PR do?

This PR adds two documentation improvements to support wallet journey guidance work:

- a new section in `docs/developing.md` for simulating wallet journey states in regtest
- a new `docs/wallet-state-friction-audit.md` covering route-level friction around fee-config blockers, coinjoin preconditions, and empty-wallet states

The regtest guide gives contributors a practical way to verify:
- empty wallet
- funded wallet
- pending confirmation
- fee config incomplete
- coinjoin ready

The friction audit summarizes where users can currently get blocked without a clear next step and outlines small, incremental opportunities for improvement.

## Motivation / Context

The codebase already contains most of the relevant wallet and service checks, but contributor guidance for reproducing these states was missing, and the current UX friction is spread across several routes.

This PR improves contributor onboarding and provides a clearer baseline for follow-up wallet guidance work.

## Dependencies

- No code changes
- No runtime changes
- No new dependencies

## Risk

Low. This PR is documentation-only.

## Visual Demo (For contributors especially)

#### Image Demo (if applicable):

Not applicable for this PR because it only adds documentation.

prs.title: feat(wallet): add data-journey-state to MainWalletPage with tests
prs.body: ## What does this PR do? This PR adds a derived `data-journey-state` attribute to the main wallet container in `MainWalletPage`. It also: - extracts the journey-state derivation into a small pure helper - derives the state from existing wallet and service signals only - adds unit tests for the derivation logic - adds React Testing Library coverage for the rendered attribute - adds an explicit precedence test to ensure `service-offline` wins over `no-wallet` This is an additive UI-only change intended to make follow-up state-aware guidance work easier to implement and test. It does not change backend behavior or existing wallet flows. ## Motivation / Context Jam already has the wallet and service state needed to identify common first-run situations, but that state is not currently surfaced in one predictable place on the main wallet route. Exposing a deterministic journey-state marker is a small, low-risk step toward better onboarding and contextual wallet guidance. ## Dependencies - No backend or API changes - No new dependencies ## Risk Low. This change only adds a DOM attribute and pure derivation logic, with unit and RTL coverage. ## Visual Demo (For contributors especially) #### Image Demo The screenshot below shows the main wallet page with Chrome DevTools open and the main wallet container selected in the Elements panel. The selected node renders: - `data-journey-state="empty-wallet"` This demonstrates that the derived journey state is present in the DOM and available for follow-up guidance work, even though this PR does not introduce a visible UI change yet.

prs.title: fix: resolve react/no-multi-comp lint errors in test files
prs.body:
**Changes Made**

- Created src/plugin/tests/components/ directory and extracted all mock components into separate files.
- **Refactored:**

```
registry.spec.ts
routes/PluginRouteRenderer.spec.tsx
routes/PluginRoutes.spec.tsx
```

- Removed unused imports and improved interface naming conventions.
- Ensured all test files now import external mock components instead of defining them inline.

**Testing**

- All tests passing successfully.
- No react/no-multi-comp lint errors (pnpm run lint:check | grep "react/no-multi-comp" → no results).

TestWrapper.tsx

- Updated affected test files to use imports:

```

routes/PluginRouteRenderer.spec.tsx
routes/PluginRoutes.spec.tsx
```

This refactor keeps tests cleaner, reusable, and easier to maintain going forward.

prs.title: feat: automate submodule releases using GitHub Actions (dry-run mode)
prs.body:
## Pull Request Template

**Description:**

- Adds a GitHub Actions workflow to automate submodule releases when a new GoFr tag (major/minor) is published.
- Addresses issue #2528.
- Implements the repository_dispatch approach to detect changed submodules and simulate release triggers in dry-run mode. This enables maintainers to review the logic safely before enabling real dispatch calls.

**Breaking Changes (if applicable):**

- No breaking changes.
- All dispatch calls are intentionally kept in dry-run mode, so no existing workflows or modules are affected.


**Additional Information:**

- Workflow file location: `.github/workflows/release-submodules.yml`

**Example dry-run output**:

- Would trigger dispatch to: gofr-dev/datasources
- Would trigger website workflow for new modules: pkg/example-module
- DISPATCH_TOKEN will be required as a secret for actual API dispatch calls (once approved).

**Checklist:**

-   [ ✅ ] I have formatted my code using  `goimport`  and  `golangci-lint`.
-   [ ] All new code is covered by unit tests.
-   [ ✅ ] This PR does not decrease the overall code coverage.
-   [ ✅ ] I have reviewed the code comments and documentation for clarity.

**Thank you for your contribution!**

prs.title: draft: add workflow to automate submodule releases using repository_dispatch
prs.body:
Fixes #2528

**Description:**

This PR introduces a draft workflow to automate submodule releases when a new GoFr tag (major/minor) is published.  
It follows the repository_dispatch approach confirmed in the issue discussion.

The workflow currently:
- Detects current and previous tags
- Identifies changed submodule directories since last release
- Maps changed directories to respective submodule repositories
- Prepares a dispatch mechanism for:
  • existing modules  
  • newly added modules → website trigger (`gofr-dev/website`)

The workflow is currently set to `workflow_dispatch` for safe testing.  
Once validated, it can be switched to run on `release.published`.


**Breaking Changes (if applicable):**

None.  
This is an additive feature with no impact on existing functionality.  
The dispatch logic and API calls are in *dry-run mode* (commented out) to enable structured review.


**Additional Information:**

- Workflow file path:  
  `.github/workflows/release-submodules.yml`

- `DISPATCH_TOKEN` will be required as a GitHub secret to trigger repository_dispatch events.

- Submodule mapping is stored in `submodule_map.txt` and can be expanded based on real repository structure.

- The dispatch calls are commented until flow is reviewed:
  ```bash
  # curl -X POST ...

prs.title: fix: replace invalid expression in dependabot config

labels.name: meta
labels.description: Issues and PRs related to the general management of the project.

labels.name: author ready
labels.description: PRs that have at least one approval, no pending requests for changes, and a CI started.

prs.body:
Dependabot was failing to parse `.github/dependabot.yml` because `open-pull-requests-limit` was set using a GitHub Actions expression. Dependabot doesn't evaluate expressions and only accepts an integer, so the config was considered invalid.

This change replaces the expression with a number. The YAML now matches Dependabot’s schema and validates without errors.

Fixes: https://github.com/nodejs/node/issues/60637

prs.body:
This PR adds the complete set of LinuxDroid branding assets, along with a usage guide for contributors, addressing issue #43.

Included:
- Banner logo (PNG + SVG)
- Horizontal logo (PNG + SVG)
- Transparent icon-only version
- Vector-friendly scalable versions

Folder Structure
```
assets/
└── logos/

    ├── Banner-logo.png
    ├── Horizontal-logo.svg
    ├── Horizontal-logo.png
    ├── no-bg-logo.png
    └── clear-bg-logo.svg
```

The logos are suitable for documentation, website headers, social media, and merchandise.

prs.title: Implement combination sum algorithm
prs.body:
Problem:
Given an array of distinct integers nums and a target integer target, return a list of all unique combinations of nums where the chosen numbers sum to target. You may return the combinations in any order. The same number may be chosen from nums an unlimited number of times. Two combinations are unique if the frequency of at least one of the chosen numbers is different. The test cases are generated such that the number of unique combinations that sum up to the target is less than 150 combinations for the given input.

Constraints
1≤nums.length≤30
1≤nums[i]≤40
All elements of nums are distinct.
1≤target≤40

prs.title: Implement function to find target sum subsets
prs.body:
Implementation:
You are given an integer array nums of size n and a target integer target. Your task is to find and return all the subsets of nums such that the sum of the elements in each subset is equal to target.

Constraints:
1≤n≤20 
1≤nums[i]≤100  
1≤target≤10^5

prs.title: Implement function to count potential pivots
prs.body:
Problem:
You are given an array arr of n distinct positive integers. This array represents the state of an original array after performing some partitioning steps of the QuickSort algorithm. Your task is to count how many elements could have been chosen as pivots during these steps to transform the original array into the current array arr.

Constraints:
1 ≤ n ≤ 10^5
1 ≤ arri ≤ 10^5

prs.title: Implement function to find k-permutations
prs.body:
Problem:
You are given an array nums consisting of distinct integers, and an integer k.
Your task is to generate all possible permutations of length k using the elements of nums.
Each element can be used at most once in each permutation.
Return all such permutations in any order.

Output:
Return all such permutations in any order.

Constraints:
1≤n,k≤8
−50≤nums[i]≤50

prs.title: Add flexible undo increment function
prs.body:
Implement flexible undo functionality for increment operations.

Problem:
You are maintaining an array nums of size n, initially filled with zeroes. You're given q operations, each of the following two types: Increment Operation 1 i: Increase the value at index i by 1. Undo Operation 2 k: Undo the last k increment operations in reverse order. If there are fewer than k increment operations to undo, undo all of them. Undo operations are guaranteed to be valid — you will never be asked to undo more increments than have occurred. Your task is to simulate all operations and print the final state of the nums array.


Output
Print the final state of the array nums.

Constraints
1≤n,q,k≤10^5
0≤nums[i]≤10^9
0≤i<n

prs.title: Absolute Degree Max
prs.body:
Problem:
Given a directed graph with n vertices and m edges, determine and print the maximum absolute difference between the in-degree and out-degree of any vertex in the graph.

Constraints:
1 ≤ n ≤ 100
0 ≤ e ≤ n* (n-1) / 2

prs.title: Difficulty Estimator Improvements & Doubts Page Enhancements
prs.body:

- Improved error handling for failed answer submissions.
- Added debug logging to assist with troubleshooting backend issues.
- Provided guidance for resolving missing dependencies and backend/server errors.

prs.title: Update README.md
prs.body: Modified by adding detailed information and uploading UI/ux screenshots for issue no. nst-sdc#153. Please let me know if any modifications are required.

prs.title: Difficulty badge notes card + Difficulty option to choose while uploading notes
prs.body: This PR implements the following features and fixes for issue #128: - Frontend: 1. Displays the difficulty label (Basic, Intermediate, Advanced) as a colored badge on each notes card in the browse page. 2. Ensures the badge is visually distinct and consistent for all notes, including those with legacy values. 3. Adds a dropdown to the upload page for users to select the difficulty level when uploading a note. - Backend: 1. Updates the note upload and edit routes (noteRoutes.js) to respect and save the selected difficulty value from the frontend. 2. Ensures only valid values ("Basic", "Intermediate", "Advanced") are accepted and stored. 3. Adds a fallback to estimate difficulty only if the value is missing or invalid. - How it works 1. When uploading or editing a note, users can select the difficulty level. 2. The selected value is saved in the database and shown as a colored badge on the notes card. 3. Legacy values ("Easy", "Medium", "Hard") are mapped to the new labels for display consistency.

prs.title: feat: Add "Difficulty Level" filter in Browse dropdown
prs.body: **Description:** This PR introduces a new "Difficulty Level" filter to the existing Browse dropdown, addressing issue #129. **Changes made:** - Enhanced the existing sort/filter dropdown with a new option: "Difficulty Level". - On selecting "Difficulty Level", a secondary dropdown appears with three options: 1. Basic 2. Intermediate 3. Advanced - Once a level is selected, notes are filtered based on their assigned difficulty. - Implemented clean dropdown transitions and ensured responsive design. **How to test:** 1. Navigate to the Browse page. 2. Open the existing filter dropdown and select "Difficulty Level". 3. A secondary dropdown should appear with difficulty options. 4. Selecting any difficulty should filter the notes accordingly. **UI Screenshots**

prs.title: View Summary Button in Notes Card

**Description:**
This PR implements the "View Summary" button as requested in issue #118.

- Added a new "View Summary" button below the existing "Review" and "Download" buttons on each Notes Card.
- When no summary is present, fallback text "Summary not available" is displayed.
- UI updated using Tailwind CSS for consistent spacing, layout, and responsiveness.

- Confirm that each Notes Card now displays a "View Summary" button below the other two.

prs.title: Major Project Overhaul: UI Redesign, Google Auth, Dark/Light Mode, Dashboard Favorites, Reviews & More
prs.body:
This PR represents a comprehensive update and transformation of the Notes Sharing Platform, merging the complete-change branch into the main branch. It includes a full-stack overhaul, significant new features, and critical improvements across both the backend and frontend.

Key Highlights
- UI/UX Overhaul

1. Redesigned the entire frontend for a cleaner, more user-friendly experience.
2. Improved navigation, layout consistency, and responsiveness across devices.

- Authentication

1. Integrated Google OAuth for sign-in and sign-up.
2. Enhanced flows for login, signup, and error handling.

- Theme Support

1. Added dark mode and light mode toggle.
2. Supports user preference and accessibility standards.

- Dashboard & Favorites

1. Revamped dashboard UI.
2. Users can now favorite notes for quick access and personalized experience.

- Reviews System

1. Introduced a complete reviews system.
2. Users can add, view, and manage reviews on notes.

- Backend Enhancements

1. Resolved existing merge conflicts and preserved all latest changes.
2. Added http://localhost:5175 to CORS config for smoother local development.
3. Integrated helmet middleware for enhanced HTTP security.
4. Improved backend error handling and logging patterns.
5. Refactored API responses for improved frontend compatibility.

- General Improvements

1. Cleaned and modularized codebase.
2. Improved performance, readability, and maintainability.
3. Added /health endpoint for health checks and uptime monitoring.

https://drive.google.com/file/d/1Z1_PGiN3GdWJ1Wmi107swNfNCw4Tga1E/view?usp=sharing


<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **New Features**
  * Added favorites functionality for notes, allowing users to favorite/unfavorite notes and view their favorites.
  * Introduced new pages: Notes History, Privacy Policy, Terms & Conditions, and Contact.
  * Added a Footer component and a ThemeProvider for dark/light mode support.
  * Enhanced dashboard with stats, favorites, and recent notes sections.

* **UI/UX Improvements**
  * Comprehensive redesign of color palette, gradients, and visual themes across the app for a modern look.
  * Major UI updates for Home, Login, Sign Up, Notes Browsing, Notes Upload, Doubts, Note Details, and Dashboard pages.
  * Improved navigation bar with responsive design, theme toggle, and account menu.
  * Added new animations, interactive effects, and utility classes for smoother transitions and feedback.
  * Enhanced forms with better validation, error handling, and feedback messages.
  * Custom scrollbars, cards, buttons, and glass morphism effects added for visual polish.

* **Bug Fixes**
  * Corrected date filtering for dashboard stats.
  * Fixed authentication checks and download handling for note files.

* **Chores**
  * Updated CORS settings to allow additional local origins.
  * Improved code style, formatting, and consistency throughout the codebase.
  * Added meta tags to prevent browser caching.

* **Documentation**
  * Updated and clarified privacy and terms content for end-users.

prs.title: Fixed Bugs
prs.body:
Hi, 
This PR resolves issue #106.
Now, the user can provide answers to doubts and also edit or delete their message.
I have also modified the backend code by installing relevant packages.

prs.title: Build Dashboard - Both Frontend and Backend
prs.body:
Designed and implemented a complete user dashboard from scratch. It fetches real-time user statistics, shows personalized data, and provides seamless navigation, all within a clean, responsive UI, addressing issue #73 and a follow-up issue altogether.

### Features Implemented
**Backend**
- Added new /api/auth/dashboard-stats endpoint
- Returns user statistics: uploaded notes, downloaded notes, reviews, monthly stats, average ratings
- Enhanced JWT middleware to support both ID and userId
- Optimized MongoDB queries for accurate activity tracking

**Frontend**
- Dashboard displays:
- Real username and formatted join date
- Stats: uploads, downloads, reviews received with average ratings
- Profile icon using Dicebear
- Loading spinner with smooth fallback and retry support
- Error handling for invalid tokens or failed API calls
- Direct navigation links to Upload, Browse, and Reviews
- Fully responsive design with a clean card layout

**Files Created & Modified**
- frontend/src/pages/Dashboard.jsx — complete dashboard page
- backend/routes/authRoutes.js — added new stats route
- backend/middlewares/authMiddleware.js — updated token handling
- backend/server.js — route registration updated

**Fixes and Improvements**
- Resolved 404 errors for the dashboard endpoint
- Fixed JWT decoding bugs and route conflicts
- Corrected MongoDB query syntax

**Testing**
- Dashboard loads dynamic user data correctly
- Handles API/network/auth errors smoothly
- Responsive on all device sizes
- Navigation buttons function as expected

[https://drive.google.com/file/d/1DN04wGvkSo0Sd-4b0u0iafPhS-41eSBO/view?usp=sharing](url)

Fully functional, maintainable, and integrated without breaking existing features.

Unauthorized delete attempts are blocked with a 403 Forbidden response.

prs.title: Add NotesBrowsingPage with owner-only delete functionality
prs.body:
This PR resolves issue #58, which helps to ensure that only authenticated users can securely delete their own uploaded notes.

### How to Test
1. Start both frontend (`npm run dev`) and backend (`npm start`)
2. Navigate to `/browse` route
3. Try:
   - Searching by title, subject, or username
   - Filtering by subject
   - Sorting notes by newest/popular/title
   - Deleting your own uploaded note
4. Upload new notes via the upload page and verify they appear here

This feature brings a clean and interactive way for users to explore shared notes on the platform and sets a solid foundation for future community-based features like ratings, favorites, and comments.

prs.title: refactor Codebase: Replace jQuery with Vue.js in Key Components
prs.body:
Fixes #433

#### Describe the changes you have made in this PR -
Migrated from jQuery to Vue.js Native Methods:

All jQuery selectors like $('.messageBox .v-card-text') have been replaced with Vue’s refs for direct DOM manipulation.
Replaced jQuery event handling methods (e.g., $('.button').on('click', ...)) with Vue’s @click, @input, and other event listeners that integrate with Vue’s reactivity system.
Refactored the truth table generation and Boolean function manipulation logic to use Vue's ref and reactive for managing the data, eliminating the need for jQuery.
Leveraged Vue’s Reactive System:

State management is now handled using Vue’s reactive data (ref and reactive), replacing the previous jQuery-based state handling.
The generation and display of the table content have been refactored to use Vue’s templating system and list rendering (v-for), making the code more efficient and easier to maintain.
Replaced jQuery’s Iteration Methods:

Instead of using jQuery's $.each for looping through arrays, I’ve switched to using JavaScript’s modern array methods like map, forEach, and join.
Improved Functionality:

The printBooleanTable function and the overall table creation process have been refactored to make better use of Vue's built-in features.
Instead of using jQuery for dynamic DOM updates, Vue’s v-if and v-for directives are now used to render table rows and headers based on the data.
Code Modernization:

Replaced traditional for loops with more modern for...of loops and used template literals for better readability and performance.
In ApplyThemes.vue, I’ve optimized the theme handling logic by removing jQuery's class manipulation methods and replacing them w

### Screenshots of the changes (If any) -



Note: Please check **Allow edits from maintainers.** if you would like us to assist in the PR.

* **Refactor**
  * Enhanced theme selection and application logic for improved reliability.
  * Improved handling of theme preference persistence.

issues.title: Add per-prefix source provenance trace for auditability of final ASMap decisions
issues.body:
### Problem
Kartograf currently collapses all inputs into a final `prefix -> ASN` mapping, but does not persist the decision lineage for each selected prefix.

That makes difficult questions hard to answer during review and incident response:
- Which source produced this mapping first (`RPKI`, `IRR`, `RouteViews`)?
- What competing candidates existed?
- What tie-breaker rule was applied?
- Which raw input artifact hash can reproduce the exact decision?

For a security-sensitive mapping pipeline, this is a major auditability gap.

### Why this matters
When a mapping is disputed (hijack suspicion, noisy IRR object, multi-origin ambiguity), maintainers currently cannot provide machine-verifiable evidence for why a specific ASN won.
This slows review, weakens trust in reproducibility, and increases operational risk of shipping silently wrong mappings.

### Proposed change
Add a provenance output artifact, e.g. `out/<epoch>/decision_trace.jsonl`, with one record per final prefix:

```json
{
  "prefix": "203.0.113.0/24",
  "chosen_asn": "AS64496",
  "chosen_source": "rpki",
  "candidates": [
    {"asn":"AS64496","source":"rpki","valid_since":...,"valid_until":...},
    {"asn":"AS64497","source":"irr","last_modified":...},
    {"asn":"AS64498","source":"routeviews","raw_line":"..."}
  ],
  "tiebreak_reason": "newer_valid_until_then_lower_asn",
  "input_artifacts": {
    "rpki_raw_sha256": "...",
    "irr_bundle_sha256": "...",
    "routeviews_sha256": "..."
  }
}
```

### Suggested implementation points
- `kartograf/rpki/parse.py`: keep candidate metadata (not just final `output_cache[prefix]`).
- `kartograf/irr/parse.py`: attach `source`, `last-modified`, and file identity to candidates.
- `kartograf/collectors/parse.py`: record multi-origin / AS-SET handling path.
- `kartograf/merge.py`: preserve provenance when base and extra sources are combined.
- Add tests under `tests/` for deterministic provenance output and tie-break explanations.

### Acceptance criteria
- Deterministic `decision_trace.jsonl` for identical input artifacts.
- Every final prefix includes a non-empty `tiebreak_reason`.
- Trace links to hashed inputs used in the run.
- Reproduction run validates both final map hash and trace hash.

issues.title: v2: avoid state updates during render in session/wallet providers (StrictMode warning + rerender risk)
issues.body:
### Summary
In v2, there are render-phase state updates in core app flow. This can cause React warnings and unstable rerender behavior (especially in StrictMode/dev), and may contribute to hard-to-debug UI state issues during rescan/wallet refresh transitions.

### Current behavior
State is updated directly inside component render paths instead of in `useEffect`:

- `src/context/JamSessionInfoContextProvider.tsx` around the `shouldUpdateRescanInfo` check:
  - `setRescanInfo(...)` is called during render when query data updates.
- `src/App.tsx` in `WalletInfoAutoReload`:
  - `setPreviousRescanning(...)` and `setRescanningFinished(...)` are called in render when rescan status changes.

### Why this matters
Calling state setters during render can lead to:
- extra/unpredictable rerenders
- React warnings (`Cannot update a component while rendering...`)
- potential update-loop edge cases in transition-heavy flows (rescan start/finish, wallet info refresh)

### Expected behavior
All state transitions should be done in effects (`useEffect`) or derived values, not during render.

### Suggested fix
1. Move `setRescanInfo(...)` logic in `JamSessionInfoContextProvider` into a `useEffect` with proper guards for stale query data.
2. Move rescan transition handling in `WalletInfoAutoReload` into `useEffect` (or derive `rescanningFinished` safely from previous/current values).
3. Keep existing stale-data protection behavior.
4. Add unit tests for transitions:
   - `rescanning: true -> false`
   - stale query data should not overwrite newer local state.

### Repro hints
- Run app in dev (React StrictMode enabled).
- Trigger rescan flow and observe console warnings/rerender patterns around session/wallet refresh.

- Repo: `joinmarket-webui/jam`
- Branch: `devel`
